93d9fc89a2e1be2b405d8e88.passage_1: Maureen O'Sullivan was an Irish-American actress best known for her role as Jane Parker in the Tarzan film series during the 1930s and 1940s. She starred opposite Johnny Weissmuller, who played Tarzan, and became widely recognized for her portrayal of the character.
93d9fc89a2e1be2b405d8e88.passage_2: The character Jane, Tarzan's companion, was famously portrayed by Maureen O'Sullivan in a series of films starting with 'Tarzan the Ape Man' in 1932. O'Sullivan's performance helped define the role of Jane in popular culture and contributed to the enduring popularity of the Tarzan franchise.

c0b531ff7508d35f7df969b7.passage_1: Clover Day is observed annually on March 17. It is a day dedicated to celebrating the clover plant, which is often associated with good luck and prosperity.
c0b531ff7508d35f7df969b7.passage_2: March 17 is recognized as Clover Day in various communities. The date coincides with the traditional symbolism of the clover, particularly the shamrock, which is linked to cultural and botanical significance.

af9976b8320692ae2426d94b.passage_1: In the United States, individuals can begin to withdraw funds from their Individual Retirement Account (IRA) without incurring a penalty starting at age 59½. Withdrawals made before this age are generally subject to a 10% early withdrawal penalty in addition to regular income taxes, unless an exception applies.
af9976b8320692ae2426d94b.passage_2: The age 59½ is a key milestone for IRA holders, as it marks the earliest age at which distributions can be taken without penalty. This rule applies to both traditional IRAs and Roth IRAs, although Roth IRA contributions can be withdrawn tax- and penalty-free at any time under certain conditions.

e66ea37d4786db37358e145b.passage_1: Kamala Harris is an American politician and attorney who is of mixed racial heritage. Her mother was an Indian-American scientist, and her father is a Jamaican-American economist, making Harris both Black and South Asian by descent.
e66ea37d4786db37358e145b.passage_2: Kamala Harris identifies as a Black woman and has spoken publicly about her experiences as a person of African American heritage. She is recognized as the first Black woman to serve as Vice President of the United States.

13ae3fdee0fa4d8f3b5bb1c8.passage_1: Roxbury is a township located in Morris County, New Jersey. It is part of the New York metropolitan area and is known for its suburban residential communities and local parks.
13ae3fdee0fa4d8f3b5bb1c8.passage_2: Morris County is situated in the northern part of New Jersey and includes several municipalities, including Roxbury Township. The county is recognized for its historical sites, recreational areas, and proximity to major urban centers.

c0bb68918e77965262bb88d6.passage_1: Tears are the clear, salty liquid produced by the lacrimal glands located above each eyeball. They serve to lubricate the eye, remove irritants, and provide nutrients to the cornea. When a person cries, tears flow from the eyes as an emotional response or due to irritation.
c0bb68918e77965262bb88d6.passage_2: The human eye produces tears to maintain eye health and comfort. Tears consist primarily of water, along with oils, mucus, and antibodies. During crying, an increased amount of tears is released, which can flow down the cheeks or evaporate from the eye surface.

2e68997119ab1d42b4710f1f.passage_1: The Epic of Gilgamesh is an ancient Mesopotamian poem that is traditionally divided into twelve tablets. These tablets collectively narrate the adventures and quests of Gilgamesh, the king of Uruk, and his companion Enkidu.
2e68997119ab1d42b4710f1f.passage_2: The standard version of the Epic of Gilgamesh consists of twelve tablets, which were discovered in the library of Ashurbanipal in Nineveh. Each tablet contains a portion of the story, ranging from Gilgamesh's heroic exploits to his search for immortality.

07fce3dfe6029acfe6a65abe.passage_1: Berne is a town located in Albany County, New York. It is situated in the western part of the county and is part of the Capital District region of the state.
07fce3dfe6029acfe6a65abe.passage_2: Albany County is one of the counties in the state of New York, and it includes several towns and cities, among them the town of Berne. The county seat of Albany County is the city of Albany.

b567e659fd24e71e8794a080.passage_1: Claysville is a borough located in the state of Pennsylvania. It is situated within Washington County, which is part of the Pittsburgh metropolitan area.
b567e659fd24e71e8794a080.passage_2: Washington County is a county in the southwestern region of Pennsylvania. Claysville is one of the municipalities that lie within the boundaries of Washington County.

34ba71def45bfd448116cb7e.passage_1: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) is a medical condition characterized by the formation of a blood clot in a deep vein, usually in the legs. It is primarily a vascular disorder related to blood clotting and circulation rather than a hemolytic condition.
34ba71def45bfd448116cb7e.passage_2: Hemolytic conditions involve the destruction of red blood cells, leading to hemolysis. DVT does not involve the breakdown of red blood cells and is classified as a thrombotic disorder, not a hemolytic one.

32198293eaf1badc594bef78.passage_1: Van Horn is a town located in Culberson County in the state of Texas. It serves as the county seat of Culberson County, which is situated in the western part of Texas.
32198293eaf1badc594bef78.passage_2: Culberson County is a county in Texas known for its sparse population and large geographic area. The town of Van Horn is the administrative center of Culberson County, providing local government services to the surrounding region.

cdf690102f83d803d6fb7f44.passage_1: Gram-negative bacteria are characterized by their cell wall structure, which includes a thin peptidoglycan layer and an outer membrane. During Gram staining, these bacteria do not retain the crystal violet stain and instead take up the counterstain, appearing pink under a microscope.
cdf690102f83d803d6fb7f44.passage_2: In the Gram staining procedure, gram-negative cells lose the initial purple dye after the alcohol decolorization step. They then absorb the safranin counterstain, resulting in a pink coloration that distinguishes them from gram-positive cells, which stain purple.

9c05f3686ca58b2ee38936ed.passage_1: Ireland is a parliamentary democracy and does not have a monarchy. The country is officially known as the Republic of Ireland and operates under a president as the head of state, who is elected by the people.
9c05f3686ca58b2ee38936ed.passage_2: The political system of Ireland is a republic, which means it does not have a king or queen. The Irish government is led by a prime minister, known as the Taoiseach, and the president serves as a largely ceremonial head of state.

353f2907dc9bc694035e97c4.passage_1: Cockroaches are omnivorous insects, meaning they consume a wide variety of food sources including plant material, decaying organic matter, and sometimes small animals or other insects. Their diet is highly adaptable, allowing them to survive in diverse environments.
353f2907dc9bc694035e97c4.passage_2: While cockroaches may occasionally eat meat or other insects, they are not strictly carnivorous. They primarily feed on organic debris, starches, sugars, and other plant-based materials, making them opportunistic feeders rather than specialized carnivores.

8cff4507ab48feb8d685249a.passage_1: Liquid asphalt is classified as a hazardous material due to its chemical properties and potential health risks. It can emit harmful fumes and poses fire hazards, requiring careful handling and transportation under hazardous materials regulations.
8cff4507ab48feb8d685249a.passage_2: In transportation and safety regulations, liquid asphalt is designated as a hazardous material (hazmat). This classification ensures that appropriate precautions are taken to prevent accidents and exposure during storage, handling, and shipping.

2929c725736e294ba5911779.passage_1: Shepherdsville is a city located in the state of Kentucky, United States. It serves as the county seat of Bullitt County, which is situated in the north-central part of the state.
2929c725736e294ba5911779.passage_2: Bullitt County is one of the counties in Kentucky, with Shepherdsville as its administrative center. The county is part of the Louisville metropolitan area and is known for its historical significance and growing communities.

8a7c59544507a8109cad3b82.passage_1: Chlamydia is a common sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. It is primarily transmitted through sexual contact involving the genital, anal, or oral mucous membranes. Transmission through casual contact such as kissing is not considered a mode of spread for chlamydia.
8a7c59544507a8109cad3b82.passage_2: The bacterium responsible for chlamydia infects the mucous membranes of the reproductive tract and sometimes the throat or eyes. While oral sex can transmit chlamydia to the throat, simple kissing does not provide the necessary conditions for the bacteria to be passed between individuals. Therefore, chlamydia is not spread by kissing alone.

089af2f1ef393db67c5c0ce6.passage_1: The Baltimore Orioles won the World Series in 1983, marking their third championship title. They defeated the Philadelphia Phillies in a series that concluded with a 4-1 victory. This win remains a significant achievement in the franchise's history.
089af2f1ef393db67c5c0ce6.passage_2: In 1983, the Baltimore Orioles secured the World Series championship by defeating the Philadelphia Phillies. This victory was the Orioles' last World Series win to date, highlighting a successful season for the team in Major League Baseball.

021b5964ab2d8d09eecc9953.passage_1: Hernán Cortés was a Spanish Conquistador who led an expedition that caused the fall of the Aztec Empire. His forces, allied with various indigenous groups, captured the Aztec capital of Tenochtitlán in 1521, effectively conquering the Aztec people and their territory.
021b5964ab2d8d09eecc9953.passage_2: The Aztecs were a Mesoamerican culture that dominated central Mexico before the arrival of the Spanish. Hernán Cortés's conquest of the Aztec Empire marked a significant event in the Spanish colonization of the Americas, leading to the establishment of New Spain.

4c0631992092090592bad22d.passage_1: Andrew Jackson was a prominent political figure in the early 19th century United States who ran for president in the elections of 1824, 1828, and 1832. His campaigns were marked by his appeal to the common man and his opposition to the established political elite.
4c0631992092090592bad22d.passage_2: Jackson first sought the presidency in 1824 but did not win the election despite having the most popular and electoral votes. He successfully won the presidency in the 1828 election and was re-elected in 1832, serving two terms as the seventh President of the United States.

f5cf5542a7adc7dbe17a37dc.passage_1: Pitch is a perceptual property of sounds that allows their ordering on a frequency-related scale. The frequency of a sound wave, measured in hertz (Hz), corresponds directly to the pitch perceived by the human ear, with higher frequencies producing higher pitches.
f5cf5542a7adc7dbe17a37dc.passage_2: Hertz (Hz) is the unit of frequency that measures the number of cycles per second of a sound wave. Since pitch is determined by the frequency of the sound wave, hertz effectively quantify the pitch of a sound, making it a standard measure in acoustics and audio engineering.

d1d39aa8098f601210a743d5.passage_1: Dobson is a town located in the state of North Carolina, United States. It serves as the county seat of Surry County, which is situated in the northwestern part of the state.
d1d39aa8098f601210a743d5.passage_2: Surry County is a county in North Carolina known for its rural landscapes and small towns. Dobson is one of the municipalities within Surry County and functions as its administrative center.

138d363c7251c8f9e62ddd37.passage_1: Convection currents in the Earth's mantle are responsible for the movement of tectonic plates. These currents arise from the heat generated by the Earth's core, causing the mantle material to circulate and create forces that move the plates.
138d363c7251c8f9e62ddd37.passage_2: The process of plate tectonics is driven by convection currents within the semi-fluid asthenosphere beneath the rigid lithosphere. These currents generate pulling and pushing forces that can cause tectonic plates to be pulled apart at divergent boundaries.

33c9f08bae33de39295c2065.passage_1: Indus Air was an Indian airline that operated in the late 1990s. It did not receive an International Air Transport Association (IATA) code during its period of operation, indicating it was not IATA approved.
33c9f08bae33de39295c2065.passage_2: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) assigns codes to airlines that meet specific operational and safety standards. Indus Air, which ceased operations in the late 1990s, was not granted an IATA code, reflecting its lack of IATA approval.

05ddf31c320568ba0ca60886.passage_1: Color blindness is a condition that affects the ability to distinguish certain colors. It is significantly more common in males than in females due to its genetic linkage to the X chromosome. Since males have only one X chromosome, a single defective gene can cause the condition, whereas females have two X chromosomes, making the condition less likely to manifest.
05ddf31c320568ba0ca60886.passage_2: The prevalence of color blindness varies between genders, with males being more frequently affected. This difference arises because the most common forms of color blindness are inherited in an X-linked recessive pattern. As a result, males, having one X and one Y chromosome, are more susceptible to expressing the trait when they inherit the affected gene.

f867a342f0711e3e1474453a.passage_1: Texas was one of the Confederate states during the American Civil War. It seceded from the United States on February 1, 1861, and joined the Confederate States of America shortly thereafter.
f867a342f0711e3e1474453a.passage_2: As a member of the Confederacy, Texas contributed troops and resources to the Confederate war effort. The state remained under Confederate control until the end of the Civil War in 1865.

eefe202a6f883e5e2aa613ab.passage_1: Escherichia coli, commonly abbreviated as E. coli, is a type of bacteria that can be found in the environment, foods, and intestines of people and animals. Certain strains of E. coli are pathogenic and can cause diarrhea when ingested through contaminated water or food.
eefe202a6f883e5e2aa613ab.passage_2: Contaminated water is a common source of bacterial infections leading to diarrhea. E. coli bacteria, particularly enterotoxigenic and enterohemorrhagic strains, are known to cause gastrointestinal illness by producing toxins that disrupt the normal function of the intestines.

75c9341b8af60239c5973484.passage_1: Pertussis, also known as whooping cough, is a highly contagious respiratory disease caused by the bacterium Bordetella pertussis. It spreads from person to person through airborne droplets produced by coughing or sneezing.
75c9341b8af60239c5973484.passage_2: As a communicable disease, pertussis can easily transmit within communities, especially among unvaccinated individuals. Effective vaccination programs are essential to control its spread and prevent outbreaks.

5b9b2b1842309ce28dc2124f.passage_1: Marijuana and tobacco are both substances that can be smoked, but they differ in their chemical composition and health effects. Studies have shown that tobacco contains numerous harmful chemicals and carcinogens that contribute to respiratory diseases and cancer, whereas marijuana has fewer known carcinogens and is generally considered to have a lower risk profile for long-term health damage.
5b9b2b1842309ce28dc2124f.passage_2: Research comparing the harms of marijuana and tobacco indicates that tobacco use is associated with higher rates of addiction, lung disease, and mortality. While marijuana use is not without risks, including potential respiratory irritation and cognitive effects, it is generally regarded as less harmful than tobacco in terms of physical health consequences and overall toxicity.

24415cc623abc7ff809f70ce.passage_1: Canary Wharf is a major business district located in East London. It is situated within the London Borough of Tower Hamlets, which is one of the 32 boroughs that make up Greater London.
24415cc623abc7ff809f70ce.passage_2: The London Borough of Tower Hamlets encompasses the area known as Canary Wharf, a prominent financial center. Tower Hamlets is located to the east of the City of London and includes several key commercial and residential areas.

a643b43bd0205f22c429d92b.passage_1: Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his famous "I Have a Dream" speech on August 28, 1963. The speech was a defining moment of the American Civil Rights Movement and took place during the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom.
a643b43bd0205f22c429d92b.passage_2: On August 28, 1963, Martin Luther King Jr. addressed a large crowd at the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C. His speech emphasized racial equality and justice, becoming one of the most iconic speeches in American history.

b2a61430db5e26650157159b.passage_1: Lackawanna County is located in the northeastern region of Pennsylvania. The county seat and largest city within Lackawanna County is Scranton, which serves as the administrative and economic center of the area.
b2a61430db5e26650157159b.passage_2: Scranton is a city in Lackawanna County, Pennsylvania, known historically for its role in the coal mining industry. It is the principal city of the Scranton–Wilkes-Barre–Hazleton metropolitan area and functions as the county's governmental hub.

ac2b5d3ce88a187445801751.passage_1: Grown-ish is an American television series that airs on the Freeform network. The show is a spin-off of the series Black-ish and follows the college experiences of the Johnson family’s eldest daughter.
ac2b5d3ce88a187445801751.passage_2: Freeform is a cable channel owned by Disney that targets young adults and teenagers. It broadcasts original series such as Grown-ish, which focuses on contemporary issues faced by college students.

f5c4fa9ad8ec900be92fca8e.passage_1: An Individual Retirement Account (IRA) is a type of retirement savings account that individuals can open independently to save for retirement with tax advantages. IRAs are available in several forms, including traditional and Roth IRAs, each with different tax treatments and contribution limits.
f5c4fa9ad8ec900be92fca8e.passage_2: A 403(b) plan is a retirement savings plan offered by public schools and certain tax-exempt organizations to their employees. It functions similarly to a 401(k) plan but is specifically designed for employees of nonprofit and educational institutions, and it has different rules and contribution limits compared to an IRA.

4f4d5014448baf61f39261b1.passage_1: Light fixtures are considered fixed assets because they are tangible property used in the operations of a business and are not intended for sale. They provide long-term utility and are capitalized on the balance sheet rather than expensed immediately.
4f4d5014448baf61f39261b1.passage_2: In accounting, fixed assets include items like buildings, machinery, and equipment that have a useful life extending beyond one year. Light fixtures fall under this category as they are installed permanently and contribute to the functionality of a property over time.

ec21ccc45497ca89b5dc1e75.passage_1: Opelika is a city located in the state of Alabama, United States. It serves as the county seat of Lee County, which is situated in the eastern part of the state.
ec21ccc45497ca89b5dc1e75.passage_2: Lee County is one of the counties in Alabama and includes the city of Opelika within its boundaries. Opelika functions as an important administrative and economic center for Lee County.

38759f60137c49e51ff77a8c.passage_1: The standard frame rate for television broadcasting in the United States and other NTSC regions is 29.97 frames per second. This frame rate was established to accommodate color television signals while maintaining compatibility with existing black-and-white broadcasts.
38759f60137c49e51ff77a8c.passage_2: Broadcast television in NTSC countries typically uses a frame rate of 29.97 frames per second. This slightly reduced frame rate from 30 fps was introduced to prevent interference between the color subcarrier and the audio signal in analog TV systems.

86608ea8bd5992595bb96d77.passage_1: Signature is a private label brand owned by Safeway, a supermarket chain in the United States. The brand offers a variety of grocery products that are sold exclusively at Safeway stores and affiliated retailers.
86608ea8bd5992595bb96d77.passage_2: Safeway, a major grocery retailer, markets a range of products under the Signature brand name. These products are part of Safeway's strategy to provide customers with quality items at competitive prices through its own branded lines.
